hero.title: AI code review from repository snapshot to actionable report
hero.description: CodeReview AI turns ZIP repository snapshots into evidence-backed findings, risk scores, human confirmation states, and exportable reports so teams see production risk before they ship.

announcement.title: Upload a repository snapshot and start reviewing

hero.tip: Uploaded code is inspected as text only. Project files are not executed and dependencies are not installed.

image.alt: CodeReview AI repository review command center

image_invert.alt: CodeReview AI repository review command center

logos.title: Designed around the objects that matter in code review

stats.title: Scope, budget, and decisions are visible up front
stats.description: Before users click start, they know how many credits the review costs, how much code can be indexed, and what output they will receive.

items.description: review modes for standard, deep, and security-focused checks

items.title: 25 MB
items.description: maximum ZIP snapshot size for controlled review scope
files indexed per repository snapshot before filtering

introduce.title: Rules create evidence first, then AI reasons across files
introduce.label: Review capabilities
introduce.description: Deterministic rules capture explainable signals first. The LLM then reasons across repository structure, auth, payments, storage, and data flow before the report becomes confirmable and exportable.

items.title: Rule scan
items.description: Finds credential patterns, dynamic execution, static-analysis suppressions, empty catches, unresolved markers, and duplicated code blocks.

items.title: Repository reasoning
items.description: Reviews related API, auth, permission, payment, schema, and model files in shared context.

items.title: Human confirmation
items.description: Mark findings as open, needs review, ignored, or fixed. Exports always reflect the latest state.

items.title: Visible budget
items.description: The workspace shows current credits, review cost, and remaining balance before the run starts.

platform.title: Upload, storage, and credits stay inside product guardrails
platform.description: A code review product must not only find bugs. It also needs users to trust upload boundaries, billing behavior, and report ownership.

image.alt: Auth, upload, storage, and user-scoped safeguards

items.title: Review requires sign-in
items.description: The workspace and review APIs depend on session identity. Detail, export, and triage routes check ownership.

items.title: Uploads are user-scoped
items.description: Storage keys use a user prefix, and uploads are limited by file count, size, and MIME type.

items.title: Credit budget is checked first
items.description: The server computes review cost by mode and returns a structured pricing path when balance is insufficient.

workflow.label: Workflow
workflow.title: Four steps to a traceable repository review
workflow.description: Every stage is designed for less noise, clearer evidence, and faster engineering decisions.

items.title: 1. Submit snapshot
items.description: Upload a ZIP repository snapshot and choose standard, deep, or security-focused review.

items.title: 2. Index safely
items.description: Filter irrelevant files and unsafe paths, while recording reviewed versus ignored files.

items.title: 3. Generate findings
items.description: Rule scan and cross-file LLM reasoning produce findings, evidence, and fixes.

items.title: 4. Confirm and export
items.description: Engineers confirm status and export Markdown or JSON reports for follow-up.

review_modes.title: The result is not a screenshot. It is a reusable review workflow.
review_modes.description: Each stage has a visible output so teams know where a finding came from, why it matters, and what to do next.

items.title: Rule scan catches deterministic risk first
items.description: Useful for credential patterns, risky execution, unresolved markers, duplicated logic, and basic engineering standards.

items.title: Cross-file context exposes systemic issues
items.description: Auth, API, database, payment, and storage files are reviewed in shared context.

items.title: Human confirmation turns findings into team memory
items.description: Confirmation state is persisted and immediately reflected in risk scores and exports.

benefits.title: Four product guardrails that matter before commercialization
benefits.label: User safeguards
benefits.description: Auth, data, credits, and payment are not back-office details. They are the reason users can trust the product with a repository.

items.title: Auth and permission boundaries
items.description: Signed-out users are redirected to sign in. Review creation, detail views, exports, and triage updates are scoped to the current user.

items.title: Data storage and upload constraints
items.description: Uploads are constrained by count, type, and size, and storage paths are user-scoped to reduce accidental exposure.

items.title: Credit management
items.description: Balance is checked before review, consumed in a transaction after success, and written to traceable credit records.

items.title: Payment flow
items.description: Prices are read server-side, callbacks validate order ownership, and notifications are handled idempotently.

use_cases.title: Bring code review into real team workflows
use_cases.description: The product avoids extra ceremony and gives clear decisions at release, security, and handoff moments.

items.title: Pre-release gate
items.description: Quickly confirm risky changes, unhandled failures, and critical payment or auth code before deploy.

items.title: Security-first review
items.description: Focus on auth, permissions, data leaks, injection surfaces, payment callbacks, and exposed APIs.

items.title: Founder team handoff
items.description: Share risk scores, confirmation states, and exportable reports across product, engineering, and contractor teams.

features.title: Coverage that matches real code review work
features.description: Findings are organized around the concerns senior engineers actually triage, not disconnected hints without context.

items.title: Correctness
items.description: Bugs, invalid assumptions, missing validation, and error-handling gaps.

items.title: Security and permissions
items.description: Credential exposure, injection surfaces, auth boundaries, and access-control risks.

items.title: Standards
items.description: Suppressed diagnostics, unresolved implementation markers, and maintainability conventions.

items.title: Duplicate logic
items.description: Substantial repeated code blocks that should converge behind one tested implementation.

items.title: Potential risk
items.description: Dynamic execution, swallowed failures, unsafe data flow, and operational blind spots.

items.title: Architecture and tests
items.description: Cross-module coupling, persistence boundaries, performance concerns, and missing test coverage.

testimonials.title: The team impact is immediate
testimonials.description: CodeReview AI does not replace engineering judgment. It makes that judgment faster, more focused, and easier to trace.

items.name: Engineering lead view
items.quote: Seeing risk and evidence before deciding whether to block a release is far better than guessing priority in a long list.

items.name: Security review view
items.quote: Putting auth, storage, credits, and payments into one review pass is exactly the kind of pre-launch SaaS self-check teams need.

items.name: Founder team view
items.quote: The report can be exported, findings can be confirmed, and the team does not have to explain the same risk across chat threads.

faq.title: Code review product questions
faq.description: Clear boundaries make the report easier to trust.

items.question: How are credits used?
items.answer: Standard reviews use 10 credits, security-focused reviews use 20 credits, and deep reviews use 25 credits. The workspace checks your balance before starting and consumes credits only after a successful review.

items.question: Does the service execute uploaded code?
items.answer: No. CodeReview AI reads a bounded ZIP snapshot, filters files, redacts common secret patterns, and sends selected source text to the configured review model.

items.question: What does the rule scan cover?
items.answer: It currently covers credential-like values, risky dynamic execution, raw HTML rendering, empty catch blocks, static-analysis suppressions, unresolved markers, and substantial cross-file duplication.

items.question: Why is human confirmation part of the workflow?
items.answer: Static rules and LLM reasoning can both need repository-specific context. Human status changes are persisted and immediately reflected in the risk score and exported report.

items.question: Can it review a GitHub pull request?
items.answer: The current product reviews ZIP repository snapshots. GitHub App and pull-request write-back are reserved for a later integration phase.

cta.title: Start with one repository snapshot
cta.description: Get a structured review, confirm the findings, and export a report your team can act on.
